tasks.subject: [auto-permission-mode] [P1] Remove autoMode from shared schemas, agent-runtime opts, and test-utils fixtures
tasks.description:
Delete the no-op `autoMode` field from the shared layer end-to-end. Do NOT touch `PermissionModeSchema` (keep its `'auto'` value — Phase 2 needs it). Do NOT remove `supportsAutoMode` anywhere — Phase 2 reuses the capability plumbing.

FILES TO CHANGE:
- `packages/shared/src/schemas.ts` — remove `autoMode` from `SessionSchema` (~line 114) and from `SessionSettingsSchema` (~line 142). Keep `PermissionModeSchema` and its `'auto'` member untouched.
- `packages/shared/src/agent-runtime.ts` — remove the `autoMode?` field from the `updateSession` opts type (~line 228).
- `packages/test-utils/src/fake-agent-runtime.ts` — drop `autoMode` from all mock/session fixtures so fixtures match the updated `SessionSchema`/`AgentRuntime` interface.

VERIFY: `grep -rn "autoMode" packages/shared/src packages/test-utils/src` returns nothing (note: `supportsAutoMode` will still match and is intentionally kept — confirm only the bare `autoMode` field is gone). `pnpm --filter @dorkos/shared typecheck` passes.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- `SessionSchema` and `SessionSettingsSchema` no longer declare `autoMode`.
- `PermissionModeSchema` still includes `'auto'`.
- `supportsAutoMode` plumbing remains intact in shared.
- `updateSession` opts in `agent-runtime.ts` no longer accept `autoMode`.
- `fake-agent-runtime.ts` fixtures compile against the updated schema with no `autoMode` references.
tasks.activeForm: Removing autoMode from shared schemas, agent-runtime opts, and test-utils fixtures

tasks.subject: [auto-permission-mode] [P1] Drop the auto_mode DB column and generate the drizzle DROP migration
tasks.description:
Remove the persisted `auto_mode` column and generate a numbered drizzle migration that DROPs it. Migration safety is verified: no consumer reads `auto_mode` outside the removed Phase 1 paths.

FILES TO CHANGE:
- `packages/db/src/schema/sessions.ts` line 26 — remove the column definition `autoMode: integer('auto_mode', { mode: 'boolean' })`.

GENERATE MIGRATION:
- Run `pnpm --filter @dorkos/db drizzle-kit generate` to produce a numbered `DROP COLUMN` migration file under `packages/db/drizzle/`. Inspect the generated SQL to confirm it is exactly an `ALTER TABLE ... DROP COLUMN auto_mode` (SQLite drizzle may rewrite the table — confirm no other columns are lost) and that the journal/snapshot metadata is updated.
- Commit the generated migration file and the updated drizzle snapshot.

VERIFY: server boots and reads/writes session settings without the column; `grep -rn "auto_mode" packages/db/src` returns nothing (the migration SQL referencing the dropped column is expected).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- `sessions.ts` no longer declares the `auto_mode` column.
- A new numbered migration under `packages/db/drizzle/` drops `auto_mode` and only `auto_mode`.
- The drizzle snapshot/journal is regenerated consistently.
- Server boots and persists/reads session settings without `auto_mode`.
tasks.activeForm: Dropping the auto_mode DB column and generating the drizzle DROP migration

tasks.subject: [auto-permission-mode] [P1] Remove autoMode/disableAutoMode server plumbing across routes, registry, session-store, runtime, message-sender, test-mode
tasks.description:
Delete the `autoMode` field and `disableAutoMode` SDK mapping from all server paths. Keep `supportsAutoMode` everywhere it appears (e.g. `runtime-cache.ts:73,84`) — Phase 2 reuses it; only remove the `autoMode` boolean and the `disableAutoMode` injection.

FILES TO CHANGE:
- `apps/server/src/routes/sessions.ts` — remove `autoMode` from the PATCH destructure/apply and from `applyStoredSettings` (~lines 40, 169, 182, 201).
- `apps/server/src/services/core/runtime-registry.ts` — remove `autoMode` from `SettingsRow` (~line 12), `rowToSettings` (~line 23), `pickSettings` (~line 34), and both SQL select clauses (~lines 228, 275).
- `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/claude-code/agent-types.ts` line 13 — remove `autoMode?` from `AgentSession`.
- `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/claude-code/sessions/session-store.ts` — remove `autoMode` init/hydrate/update handling (~lines 83, 113, 125, 195, 243).
- `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/claude-code/claude-code-runtime.ts` line 204 — remove `autoMode?` from the `updateSession` opts type.
- `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/claude-code/messaging/message-sender.ts` lines 272-277 — drop the `disableAutoMode` branch entirely. The settings block becomes:
  ```ts
  if (session.fastMode) { … fastMode … }
  ```
  (i.e. the surrounding settings object no longer injects `disableAutoMode: 'disable'` when `session.autoMode === false`).
- `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/test-mode/test-mode-runtime.ts` — drop `autoMode?` from its `updateSession`/opts type.

DO NOT REMOVE: `supportsAutoMode` in `runtime-cache.ts` (lines 73, 84) or anywhere else — it is kept for Phase 2 gating.

VERIFY: `grep -rn "autoMode\b" apps/server/src | grep -v supportsAutoMode` returns nothing; `grep -rn "disableAutoMode" apps/server/src` returns nothing. `pnpm --filter @dorkos/server typecheck` passes.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- No `autoMode` field or `disableAutoMode` mapping remains in any server source file.
- `supportsAutoMode` plumbing is intact in `runtime-cache.ts` and downstream.
- `message-sender.ts` settings block no longer references `disableAutoMode`; the `fastMode` branch is preserved.
- Server typechecks.
tasks.activeForm: Removing autoMode/disableAutoMode server plumbing across routes, registry, session-store, runtime, message-sender, test-mode

Remove the misplaced Auto toggle and all its client plumbing. The Model Config Mode section stays but renders ONLY the Fast toggle. Keep `supportsAutoMode` plumbing where it flows from caps (Phase 2 reuses it); only remove the `autoMode` boolean state and the `supportsAutoMode`-gated Auto toggle in the popover.

FILES TO CHANGE:
- `apps/client/src/layers/features/status/ui/ModelConfigPopover.tsx` — remove `autoMode` and `supportsAutoMode` from `ModeSectionProps`; remove the Auto `ModeToggle` (lines 240-247); remove the popover's `autoMode` and `onChangeAutoMode` props plus the related state and any `showModes` derivation that depended on the Auto toggle. `ModeSection` becomes Fast-only — KEEP the section, it renders just the Fast toggle.
- `apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/model/use-session-status.ts` — remove `SessionStatusData.autoMode` (line 19), the `localAutoMode` state (line 50), its derivation (line 78), `statusData.autoMode` (line 85), and all `updateSession`/effect-deps handling for `autoMode`.
- `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/status/ChatStatusSection.tsx` lines 290-291 — remove the `autoMode` and `onChangeAutoMode` props passed to `ModelConfigPopover`.

VERIFY: `grep -rn "autoMode\b" apps/client/src | grep -v supportsAutoMode` returns nothing (test files updated in task 1.5). `pnpm --filter @dorkos/client typecheck` passes. Open the Model Config popover — the Mode section shows only Fast, no Auto toggle.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- The Model Config Mode section shows only the Fast toggle; no "Auto" toggle.

- `use-session-status.ts` exposes no `autoMode` in `SessionStatusData` and carries no `localAutoMode` state.

tasks.subject: [auto-permission-mode] [P2] Add the 'auto' permission-mode descriptor to CLAUDE_CODE_CAPABILITIES
tasks.description:
Offer `'auto'` as a selectable permission mode by adding its descriptor to the runtime capabilities. SDK passthrough is already generic (ADR-0240): `setPermissionMode('auto')` and per-turn `permissionMode` need no further server change (confirmed by `claude-code-runtime-interactive.test.ts:625`).

FILE TO CHANGE:
- `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/claude-code/runtime-constants.ts` — add to `CLAUDE_CODE_CAPABILITIES.permissionModes.values` (the array currently holding `default`, `acceptEdits`, `plan`, `bypassPermissions`):
  ```ts
  {
    id: 'auto',
    label: 'Auto',
    description:
      'A safety classifier approves or denies tool calls automatically — fewer interruptions on long autonomous runs. Research preview.',
  }
  ```
  Leave the existing `'dontAsk'` omission and its explanatory comment in place.

VERIFY: `pnpm --filter @dorkos/server typecheck` passes; a capabilities fetch now returns five permission-mode values including `auto`.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- `permissionModes.values` includes the `'auto'` descriptor with the exact id/label/description above.
- `'dontAsk'` remains omitted.
- Existing capability tests (and `claude-code-runtime-interactive.test.ts`) still pass.
tasks.activeForm: Adding the 'auto' permission-mode descriptor to CLAUDE_CODE_CAPABILITIES

tasks.subject: [auto-permission-mode] [P2] Safety-critical fallback fix in interactive-handlers.ts — auto renders approval cards, not silent auto-allow
tasks.description:
SAFETY-CRITICAL. In `'auto'` mode the classifier resolves at SDK evaluation step 3 and `canUseTool` is normally never reached. It is reached ONLY after the classifier's fallback (3 consecutive / 20 total blocks), at which point an approval card is the correct, safe response — not the current silent auto-allow.

FILE TO CHANGE:
- `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/claude-code/messaging/interactive-handlers.ts` (the `createCanUseTool` approval gate, ~line 237). Change the gate so `'auto'` is treated like `'default'`:
  ```ts
  if (session.permissionMode === 'default' || session.permissionMode === 'auto') {
    return handleToolApproval(session, context.toolUseID, toolName, input, context);
  }
  ```
  The `AskUserQuestion` routing and the `READ_ONLY_TOOLS`/`DORKOS_AGENT_TOOLS` auto-allow that precede this gate stay unchanged; the remaining non-`default`/non-`auto` modes keep their existing auto-allow.

TEST TO ADD/UPDATE (server unit):
- In the `interactive-handlers` test file, add a case asserting that when `session.permissionMode === 'auto'` and `canUseTool` is invoked for a non-read-only, non-agent tool, the handler calls `handleToolApproval` (renders an approval card) — NOT `{ behavior: 'allow' }`. Keep/confirm the existing `'default'` approval case and a case proving `acceptEdits`/`bypassPermissions` still auto-allow.

VERIFY: `pnpm vitest run` against the interactive-handlers test passes; `pnpm --filter @dorkos/server typecheck` passes.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- In `'auto'` mode, after the classifier fallback reaches `canUseTool`, tool calls raise approval cards (no silent auto-allow).
- The unit test explicitly asserts `auto` → `handleToolApproval`.
- `default` behavior and other modes' auto-allow are unchanged.
tasks.activeForm: Applying the safety-critical fallback fix in interactive-handlers.ts so auto renders approval cards

tasks.subject: [auto-permission-mode] [P2] Add permission_denied StreamEvent (schema) and map SDK system/permission_denied
tasks.description:
Surface classifier denials by adding a new StreamEvent and mapping the SDK `system/permission_denied` message to it.

SCHEMA CHANGES — `packages/shared/src/schemas.ts`:
- Add `'permission_denied'` to the `StreamEventTypeSchema` enum (the `z.enum([...])` near line 29).
- Add a new payload schema with this exact shape:
  ```ts
  { toolCallId: string; toolName: string; reasonType?: string; reason?: string; message: string }
  ```
  Define it as e.g. `PermissionDeniedEventSchema` (mirror the style of the existing `*EventSchema` definitions, with `.openapi(...)`).
- Add `PermissionDeniedEventSchema` to the `StreamEventSchema.data` union (the `z.union([...])` around line 757).

MAPPER CHANGES — `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/claude-code/sdk/event-mappers/system-event-mapper.ts`:
- Add a branch (alongside the existing `message.subtype === '…'` branches) for `message.subtype === 'permission_denied'`. The SDK message is `SDKPermissionDeniedMessage` with fields `tool_name`, `tool_use_id`, `decision_reason_type`, `decision_reason`, `message`. Map them to the new event payload:
  - `toolCallId` ← `tool_use_id`
  - `toolName` ← `tool_name`
  - `reasonType` ← `decision_reason_type` (carries `'classifier'` among other discriminators)
  - `reason` ← `decision_reason`
  - `message` ← `message`
  Yield a single `{ type: 'permission_denied', data: … }` StreamEvent. This also removes `permission_denied` from the catch-all/unknown-subtype log path so it no longer logs as an unknown subtype.

TESTS TO ADD/UPDATE (server unit):
- In the `system-event-mapper` test, add a case feeding an `SDKPermissionDeniedMessage` (`subtype: 'permission_denied'`, including `decision_reason_type: 'classifier'`) and assert it yields one `permission_denied` event with the mapped fields, and that no catch-all/unknown-subtype warning is logged.

VERIFY: `pnpm --filter @dorkos/shared typecheck`, `pnpm --filter @dorkos/server typecheck`, and `pnpm vitest run` against the system-event-mapper test all pass.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- `StreamEventTypeSchema` includes `'permission_denied'` and `StreamEventSchema.data` accepts the new payload.
- The mapper converts `system/permission_denied` to the new event with the exact field mapping above.
- `permission_denied` no longer hits the catch-all log; a unit test proves the mapping and the absence of the unknown-subtype warning.
tasks.activeForm: Adding the permission_denied StreamEvent schema and mapping SDK system/permission_denied

tasks.subject: [auto-permission-mode] [P2] Client per-model gating of 'auto' in PermissionModeItem with explanatory tooltip
tasks.description:
Show `'auto'` in the Permission Mode dropdown only when the active model reports `supportsAutoMode`; otherwise hide it behind an explanatory tooltip. `supportsAutoMode` plumbing was preserved in Phase 1.

FILES TO CHANGE:
- `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/status/ChatStatusSection.tsx` — compute `modelSupportsAutoMode` for the active model: call `useModels(sessionId)`, find the entry matching `status.model`, read its `supportsAutoMode`. Pass `modelSupportsAutoMode` down to `PermissionModeItem`.
- `apps/client/src/layers/features/status/ui/PermissionModeItem.tsx` — accept the new `modelSupportsAutoMode` prop. Filter `'auto'` out of the rendered descriptor list (sourced from `caps.permissionModes.values`) when `!modelSupportsAutoMode`. When filtered, show a tooltip with copy: "Auto mode requires Opus 4.6+ or Sonnet 4.6". The existing `MODE_ICONS.auto` (Sparkles), `MODE_WARN.auto` (red danger tint), and `FALLBACK_LABELS.auto` ('Auto') already cover icon/tint/label for the visible case.

TESTS TO ADD/UPDATE (client unit):
- `PermissionModeItem` test: assert `'auto'` is rendered when `modelSupportsAutoMode` is true and is NOT rendered (and the explanatory tooltip is present) when false.

VERIFY: `pnpm --filter @dorkos/client typecheck` and the `PermissionModeItem` vitest pass.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- `'auto'` appears in the Permission Mode dropdown only when the active model reports `supportsAutoMode`.
- When the model does not support it, `'auto'` is hidden and an explanatory tooltip ("Auto mode requires Opus 4.6+ or Sonnet 4.6") is shown.
- `ChatStatusSection` derives `modelSupportsAutoMode` from `useModels(sessionId)` and the active `status.model`.
- Unit tests cover both the supported and unsupported cases.
tasks.activeForm: Implementing client per-model gating of 'auto' in PermissionModeItem with explanatory tooltip

tasks.subject: [auto-permission-mode] [P2] Once-per-session entry confirmation modal + per-session client state for 'auto'
tasks.description:
Gate the first switch to `'auto'` in a given session behind a confirmation modal. State is client-only and per-session — no server/schema persistence.

IMPLEMENTATION:
- Add client-only per-session state, e.g. a `Set<sessionId>` of sessions that have already confirmed auto, in `session-chat-store` (the existing session/chat Zustand store), with an action to record a confirmed session and a selector to check it.
- On selecting `'auto'` in the permission-mode flow (the `PermissionModeItem` selection handler wired through `ChatStatusSection`): if the session has NOT yet confirmed, open a confirmation modal instead of applying immediately. The modal explains what the classifier blocks, labels it a "research preview", and offers Confirm / Cancel.
- On Confirm: record the session in the per-session state and apply `updateSession({ permissionMode: 'auto' })`.
- On Cancel: do nothing (mode unchanged).
- Subsequent switches to `'auto'` in the same session skip the modal and apply directly.

TESTS TO ADD (client unit):
- Assert that the first selection of `'auto'` for a session opens the modal and does NOT call `updateSession` until Confirm; Confirm records the session and calls `updateSession({ permissionMode: 'auto' })`.
- Assert that a second selection of `'auto'` for the same (already-confirmed) session applies directly without opening the modal.
- Assert Cancel leaves the mode unchanged and does not record the session.

VERIFY: `pnpm --filter @dorkos/client typecheck` and the new modal/store vitest pass.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- First switch to `'auto'` in a session shows the confirmation modal (classifier explanation + "research preview" + Confirm/Cancel).
- Confirm applies `updateSession({ permissionMode: 'auto' })` and records the session client-side.
- Subsequent switches in the same session do not show the modal.
- No server/schema persistence is added; state lives only in the client store.
tasks.activeForm: Implementing the once-per-session entry confirmation modal and per-session client state for 'auto'

tasks.subject: [auto-permission-mode] [P2] Client denial chip from permission_denied event + research-preview tag on the Auto option
tasks.description:
Render classifier denials as a read-only chip in the message stream, and add a research-preview affordance to the Auto option.

DENIAL CHIP:
- Handle the `permission_denied` StreamEvent in the chat stream handler (the SSE/stream reducer that turns StreamEvents into message-stream items). On receiving it, append a read-only "blocked" chip to the message stream, styled distinctly from user denials.
- Chip copy: "Blocked by auto-mode classifier: {reason}" (use the event's `reason`/`message`). Label it specifically as a classifier denial when `reasonType === 'classifier'`.
- The chip is read-only — no actions, no re-approval affordance.

RESEARCH-PREVIEW TAG:
- Add a small "Preview" tag on the `Auto` option in the permission-mode dropdown (`PermissionModeItem`), alongside the existing Sparkles icon + danger tint.

TESTS TO ADD (client unit):
- Stream-handler test: feeding a `permission_denied` event (with `reasonType: 'classifier'`) appends a read-only chip with the classifier-specific copy and the reason text.
- `PermissionModeItem` test: the `Auto` option renders the "Preview" tag.

VERIFY: `pnpm --filter @dorkos/client typecheck` and the new stream-handler/PermissionModeItem vitest pass.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- A `permission_denied` event renders a read-only "blocked" chip in the message stream, styled distinctly from user denials, with classifier-specific labeling when `reasonType === 'classifier'`.
- The `Auto` option shows a "Preview" tag alongside the Sparkles icon + danger tint.
- Unit tests cover the chip and the preview tag.
tasks.activeForm: Implementing the client denial chip from permission_denied events and the research-preview tag

tasks.subject: [auto-permission-mode] [P2] Verify Phase 2 end-to-end — typecheck, lint, vitest, and live dev on isolated :6242
tasks.description:
Full verification pass for Phase 2. Run all static + test gates, then validate the live behavior on an isolated dev server.

STATIC + TESTS:
- `pnpm typecheck` (repo-wide) passes.
- `pnpm lint` passes.
- `pnpm test -- --run` (Vitest single run) passes, including the new/updated suites: interactive-handlers fallback gate, system-event-mapper `permission_denied`, `PermissionModeItem` gating + preview tag, the confirmation-modal/store tests, and the denial-chip stream-handler test.

LIVE DEV (isolated, dev convention port :6242):
- Start the dev server on :6242 (do not collide with a running production cockpit on :4242).
- On a session whose active model is Opus 4.8 (supports auto): open the Permission Mode dropdown — confirm `'auto'` is visible with the Sparkles icon, danger tint, and "Preview" tag. Select it — confirm the once-per-session confirmation modal appears; Confirm it. Re-open and re-select `'auto'` — confirm the modal does NOT reappear for the same session.
- Trigger a benign action the classifier blocks — confirm a read-only "Blocked by auto-mode classifier: …" chip renders in the message stream.
- Switch the session to a non-supporting model (e.g. Haiku) — confirm `'auto'` is hidden in the dropdown with the explanatory tooltip.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- `pnpm typecheck`, `pnpm lint`, and `pnpm test -- --run` all pass.
- Live: `'auto'` visible on Opus 4.8 with icon/tint/preview; confirmation modal shows once per session; benign block renders the denial chip; `'auto'` hidden on Haiku with the tooltip.
- After the classifier's interactive fallback, tool calls raise approval cards (no silent auto-allow) — spot-checked or covered by the unit test from task 2.2.
tasks.activeForm: Verifying Phase 2 end-to-end with typecheck, lint, vitest, and live dev on isolated :6242
